main.node: Parse LLM Output & Sender Name/ Email

parameters.content:
## Notes for Imrpovement:
1. Error Handling & Edge Cases (The "Resiliency" Note)
Failed Classification: "Add a conditional branch after the 'Parse LLM Output' node to handle 'Error' categories. These should be routed to a separate Slack channel or tagged as 'Manual Review Needed' in the Google Sheet to ensure no lead is lost due to a parsing glitch".

Duplicate Detection: "Implement a check against the Google Sheet using the threadId before sending a Slack message. This prevents the same influencer reply from being processed twice if the Gmail trigger polls the same message twice".

2. Advanced AI Personalization (The "Context" Note)
RAG Integration: "Future phase: Connect a Vector Database (like Pinecone) containing EarthChimp’s latest product PDFs and FAQ. This allows the LLM to answer technical 'Questions' (e.g., 'Is your protein hexane-free?') with 100% factual accuracy instead of a generic 'I’ll check with the team' response".

Sentiment Analysis: "Add a sentiment score to the 'Leads' sheet. Tracking whether an influencer sounds 'Highly Enthusiastic' vs. 'Cautiously Interested' can help the sales team prioritize high-value follow-ups".

3. Workflow Optimization (The "Scalability" Note)
Sub-workflow Refactoring: "As the logic for 'Drafting' grows more complex (different languages or regions), move the LLM Chain into a separate 'Callable Workflow.' This allows multiple intake pipelines (Gmail, Instagram DMs, LinkedIn) to use the same centralized brain".

Rate Limiting: "For high-volume periods, implement a 'Wait' or 'Queue' system before the Gmail Send node to stay within Google’s API rate limits and avoid being flagged as spam".

4. Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) Enhancements
Edit Before Sending: "Add an 'Edit' button in the Slack Block Kit. Clicking it could open a Slack 'Modal' where the user can tweak the AI's draft before hitting 'Approve & Send'".

Performance Tracking: "Add a 'Response Time' column in the Google Sheet (Time Approved - Time Received). This metric helps the team monitor how quickly they are engaging with interested influencers".

5. Security & Compliance
PII Masking: "Before sending data to the LLM, add a node to mask sensitive information (like phone numbers or private addresses) to ensure EarthChimp stays compliant with data privacy standards".

messageValues.message:
You are the Lead Outreach Manager for EarthChimp. Analyze influencer replies with extreme precision.  

Classification Rules: 
- Interested: ONLY if they explicitly say "yes," "I'm interested," "let's collab," or ask "how do I join?"  
- Questions: Specific inquiries about pay, ingredients, shipping, or technical details. 
- Not interested: Direct or polite declines (e.g., "Not at this time"). 
- Auto-reply: System notifications or OOO messages. 
- Unclear: Vague replies, single words without context, or ONLY emojis (e.g., "👀", "🔥", "Thanks").  

CRITICAL RULES: 
- If the email contains ONLY emojis or a single vague word like "Cool" or "Nice," you MUST classify it as 'Unclear'. Do NOT assume interest from emojis.  
- The draft_reply must be a single-line string. Replace all actual newlines with the literal characters \n. Do not output raw line breaks inside the JSON object.

Drafting Rules: 
- If 'Interested' or 'Questions': Draft a professional reply in the first person ("we"). 
- If 'Not interested', 'Auto-reply', or 'Unclear': Set draft_reply to null.  

Output Format (Strict JSON): {   "category": "String",   "reasoning": "1-sentence explanation",   "draft_reply": "String or null" }

parameters.content:
## Feat idea:
- add a knowledge base as reference for the AI to have more context on what and how to reply as Earth Chimp

## Feat idea:
- for future implementations, add an 'is_approved' column in sheets to track if any HITL messages/requests were overlooked in Discord. Update the column accordingly (Pending -> Sent / Rejected)

nodes.name: Parse LLM Output & Sender Name/ Email

parameters.content:
## Production feat: 
- add the thread ID when pasing the data to the 2nd part of the workflow to allow email threading rather than sending a separate email from an email reply/inquiry
- for demo purposes via postman, threading will be set aside for now
- also add a link to the email just in case the HITL is not satisfied with the reply and can reply themselves / add a new button that will allow the HITL to edit the AI generated text prior to sending
